tasks.title: Task 1: Sign in to AWS Management Console

steps.text: Click on the Open Console button, and you will get redirected to AWS Console in a new browser tab.
On the AWS sign-in page,
Leave the Account ID as default. Never edit/remove the 12 digit Account ID present in the AWS Console. otherwise, you cannot proceed with the lab.
Now copy your User Name and Password in the Lab Console to the IAM Username and Password in AWS Console and click on the Sign in button.
Once Signed In to the AWS Management Console, Make the default AWS Region as US East (N. Virginia) us-east-1.
Note : If you face any issues, please go through FAQs and Troubleshooting for Labs.
In this task, we are going to create a Lambda function by providing the required parameters like name, runtime, execution rule etc.
Navigate to the Services menu at the top, then click on Lambda under the Compute section.
Click on Create a function button.
Select Author from Scratch
Function Name: Enter MyStepFunction_lambda
Runtime: Select Python 3.10
Under Additional settings, enable Custom execution role
Choose the Execution role
Select Lambda_role_<RANDOM_NUMBER> and click Save
Leave other options as the default.
Click on the Create function button.
Once the Lambda Function is created successfully, it will look like the screenshot below:
Under Function code:
Replace the existing code with the below python code.
import json
def lambda_handler(event, context):
# TODO: Implement your Lambda function logic here
return {
'statusCode': 200,
'body': json.dumps('You have successfully invoked the lambda function from Step Function.')
Now save the code using by clicking on the Deploy button.
Now copy your lambda function ARN from the top right corner and place it in a text editor. This arn will be used later.

tasks.title: Task 3: Create a State Machine in Step Function

steps.text: Navigate to the Services menu at the top, then click on Step Functions in the Application Integration section.
Click on the State machines from the left navigation panel.
Click on Create state machine button.
Select Create from blank. Give the name of your choice and Click on Continue.
Click on {}Code.
Definition : Replace the existing state machine JSON code with the below one.
"Comment": "Sample State machine code to invoke a lambda function",
"StartAt": "invokeLambda",
"States": {
"invokeLambda": {
"Type": "Task",
"Resource": "<YOUR_FUNCTION_ARN>",
"End": true
Now replace the entire value of Resource key with the lambda ARN that you have copied and placed in the text edit.
Now on the right side the flow diagram will be as follows :
Note : If this image is not displayed, please click the refresh button, to reload the diagram.
Now click on the Config button.
Specify details:
Name : Default MyStateMachine (This name cannot be changed here) and Select Standard as Type.
Permissions :
Execution role : select Choose an existing role
Existing roles : select StepFunction_lambda_role-RANDOM_NUMBER
Leave everything as default.
Click on the Create button.
Click on the created state machine and select Execute button.
New execution :
Enter an execution name : Enter SampleTest1
Input : leave as default.
Click on the Start execution button.
In the Graph inspector diagram, the colour of invokeLambda will become green (success) as shown in the image below.
Click on the invokeLambda step.
You will be able to see the details below on the right side column.
Now, click on the Input/Output tab. You will see the output from our lambda function.
Once the lab steps are completed, please click on the Validation button on the left side panel.
This will validate the resources in the AWS account and displays whether you have completed this lab successfully or not.
Sample output :
Do You Know?
Step Functions include built-in support for wait states, where you can pause the workflow execution for a specific duration, until a specified time, or until a specific event occurs.
Completion and Conclusion
You have successfully created a Lambda function.
You have successfully created a state machine in a step function.
You have successfully tested the step function.
End Lab
Sign out of AWS Account.
You have successfully completed the lab.
Once you have completed the steps, click on End Lab from the lab console.
